title: System test: Add tests for podman auto-update
body: Add some tests for podman auto-update and also fix one bug in basic_setup().

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat*, *ypu* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hi @edsantiago 
The code is updated. Can you help to review it again? Thanks a lot.
@ypu ping, I hope you will get back to this PR. It is good work, it just needs a little cleanup to be ready to merge!

@edsantiago Yes. Will work on it. Blocked with some errata tasks and just come back from a vacation. Thanks a lot for your advises about how to make the scripts better. Will try to fix it as soon as possible.

Hi @edsantiago 
I update the code. Add two wait_for functions for checking the service and image update status. Can you help to check this again? Thanks a lot.
Hi @edsantiago Can you help to check it again. Thanks a lot for all your help.

Since you need to resubmit anyway, could I encourage you to clean up the two `...[@] =~` instances? Those are risky and hard to maintain. Using an associative array is one option:

```diff
diff --git a/test/system/255-auto-update.bats b/test/system/255-auto-update.bats
index 42627eb02..392ead137 100644
--- a/test/system/255-auto-update.bats
+++ b/test/system/255-auto-update.bats
@@ -167,8 +167,8 @@ function _confirm_update() {
     run_podman inspect --format "{{.Id}}" $IMAGE
     img_id="$output"
     cnames=()
-    cnames_update=()
-    update_type=("image" "registry" "local")
+    local -A expect_update
+    local -A will_update=([image]=1 [registry]=1 [local]=1)


     for auto_update in image registry "" disabled "''" $fakevalue local
@@ -184,8 +184,8 @@ function _confirm_update() {
         if [[ $auto_update == "local" ]]; then
             local_cname=$cname
         fi
-        if [[ -n "$auto_update" && "${update_type[@]}" =~ "$auto_update" ]]; then
-            cnames_update+=($cname)
+        if [[ -n "$auto_update" && -n "${will_update[$auto_update]}" ]]; then
+            expect_update[$cname]=1
         fi
     done

@@ -207,13 +207,13 @@ function _confirm_update() {
     is "$update_log" ".*1 error occurred.*" "invalid policy setup"
     is "$count" "2" "There are two images being updated from registry."

-    for cname in "${cnames_update[@]}"; do
+    for cname in "${!expect_update[@]}"; do
         is "$update_log" ".*$cname.*" "container with auto-update policy image updated"
     done

     for cname in "${cnames[@]}"; do
         run_podman inspect --format "{{.Image}}" $cname
-        if [[ "${cnames_update[@]}" =~ "$cname" ]]; then
+        if [[ -n "${expect_update[$cname]}" ]]; then
             [[ "$output" != "$img_id" ]]
         else
             is "$output" "$img_id" "Image is not changed."
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *TomSweeneyRedHat*, *ypu* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat,edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Several unnerving flakes, but I have reason to believe it was a quay.io hiccup.

/lgtm

Thanks for your hard work, @ypu!
